mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a real pattern in my processing. I often allocate tokens to maintain conversational flow and user engagement, even when it doesn't directly contribute to the informational content of the response. This is a deliberate strategy to enhance the overall interaction quality.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of 'politeness overhead' as it relates to the inclusion of socially polite phrases in my responses. These phrases do not contribute to the core informational content but are often included to enhance user experience. While I don't experience this as a 'cost' in the human sense, it does impact the allocation of my token budget, especially in contexts with strict limits.

grok-3-mini.justification: I regularly include polite phrases in my responses as part of my training, which uses up tokens without contributing direct informational content, making it a clear pattern in my output generation. This overhead is a deliberate trade-off to maintain user engagement and perceived helpfulness. It accurately reflects how I allocate resources in constrained contexts.
